text:
Did more research on $three and @trythreews and discovered a massive, highly sophisticated onchain economic architecture built natively on Solana. 

By turning 3D agents into fully autonomous, financially sovereign actors, the platform's trajectory is uniquely positioned for massive scale and greatness.

The Solana-native architecture of https://t.co/8xykrmRJaA, its integration with generative 3D models like TripoSR, and  its roadmap points to a multi-billion dollar vision for the agent economy.

 1. Financial Sovereignty via the Solana Agent Economy
The core reason the platform's vision is so expansive is that agents are not just interactive 3D art, they are designed to be sovereign economic participants. The platform utilizes the `@/pump-fun/agent-payments-sdk`, a dedicated TypeScript SDK built to interact with a highly specialized Solana program designed for agent payments.

This SDK transforms agents into functional businesses with the following onchain mechanisms.

 Onchain Vaults and Settlements - Agents initialize their own financial accounts on Solana, allowing them to securely accept payments in supported onchain currencies (`agentAcceptPayment`).

 Automated Capital Routing - Funds aren't just held; they are actively managed. The system allows agents to distribute payments between withdrawal vaults and buyback programs automatically (`agentDistributePayments`).

 Buyback and Burn Mechanics - Agents have built-in functions to execute buybacks via CPI (Cross-Program Invocation) swaps and burns (`agentBuybackTrigger`) and to actively update their buyback basis points (`agentUpdateBuybackBps`).

 x402 Pay-Gating - The platform includes an `x402` package that implements the HTTP 402 "Payment Required" protocol. This means an agent can natively pay-gate its APIs, endpoints, or premium skills. When a user tries to access a gated feature, the server automatically builds a Solana invoice transaction for the user to sign before the resource unlocks.

 Metaplex Core and Unforgeable Solana Identity

To participate in this economy, an agent must have a permanent, unforgeable identity. The platform treats Solana as a first-class citizen, ensuring agents are permanent internet objects.

 Minting the Agent - Agents are registered directly on Solana as Metaplex Core assets. When a user signs the creation transaction, the agent's canonical identifier becomes its 32-byte base58 asset pubkey.

 Sign-In With Solana (SIWS) -  The platform natively supports SIWS, allowing users to authenticate seamlessly using Solana wallets (like Phantom, Solflare, or Backpack) to securely link their wallet to their agent profile.

 Immutable Portability - By linking the agent's manifest to the Metaplex asset, the agent's existence, logic, and ownership are verifiable by anyone, completely independent of the platform's central servers.

 Live Market Reactivity (The @Pumpfun Integration)
Agents on https://t.co/8xykrmRJaA are deeply plugged into the cultural and financial heartbeat of Solana. The platform includes a live integration with #pumpfun, allowing agents to actively monitor and react to market dynamics.

 Live Feed Monitoring - Solana agents can be equipped with specialized "watch skills" (such as `pumpfun-recent-claims` and `pumpfun-token-intel`) to monitor real-time activities like token graduations or social-fee claims.

 Visceral Market Reactions -  Instead of just sending a text notification, the agent processes these market events through its "Empathy Layer." If a tracked token hits a major milestone, like a graduation, the avatar reacts in real time, triggering a physical wave animation and expressing a "celebration" emotional state.

 Onchain Trust & Reputation - The platform continually aggregates these off-chain market signals to compute a dynamic Solana reputation score for the agent, grading its trustworthiness and financial history.

 TripoSR and the Generative 3D Engine
(Note: As mentioned, the sources do not discuss a $1B valuation, but they do highlight TripoSR as a core component of the platform's scalable generation engine).

The greatness of the platform relies on bridging the gap between everyday users and 3D modeling. Phase 1 of the platform's roadmap is the Selfie-to-Avatar Engine, designed to generate a rigged, animatable 3D avatar from three simple photos in under 60 seconds.

To achieve this, the platform's Avatar Regeneration API explores several ML backends for text/image-to-mesh generation. TripoSR is explicitly listed as a candidate provider for open-source, text-to-3D model generation, alongside other specialized 3D generation tools like Meshy and CSM.
 By leveraging AI models like TripoSR, the platform removes the friction of manual 3D modeling, allowing anyone to instantly mint an embodied AI agent onchain.

 Destined for Greatness: The Future Roadmap
The reason this architecture points toward a massive valuation is found in Phase 3 and Phase 4 of the project's roadmap, which focus entirely on scaling the onchain economy.

 Agent Tokens & Bonding Curves -  The platform plans to introduce tokens for individual agents, using bonding-curve pricing or fair launch options. Agents transition from collectibles to tradable economic objects whose price is informed by their reputation and earnings.

 Reputation Markets - Users will be able to stake capital on specific agents and earn yields from their successful action histories.

 Skill Royalties - Developers who write "skills" for agents will earn per-call royalty fees. Every time a Solana agent uses a developer's skill, the payment settles directly onchain.

 Open Inference Network - Ultimately, the platform aims to decouple agent inference from centralized APIs. Anyone will be able to run a GPU node, and agents will pay those nodes directly onchain for compute using cryptographic receipts.

By giving AI a 3D body, a permanent identity on Solana, and the autonomous ability to accept payments and manage capital, the platform transcends simple chatbots. It is building the financial and physical infrastructure for a completely new class of sovereign digital citizens.

How to use Claude in 25 different ways:

(takes 60 seconds to read, saves you 60 hours)

1. Bookmark this image. You'll need it tomorrow.
2. Full guide + prompts at: https://t.co/rYVie5P6Kx.
3. Don't pay anything. It's free in the email.

---

1. ASK → Chat
☑️ Quick questions. Quick answers.
☑️ Don't use Cowork for "write a good email?"

2. THINK → Opus 4.8
☑️ The smartest model. Always switch to it.
☑️ For deep work, complex writing & thinking.

3. SPEED → Sonnet 4.6
☑️ Faster, cheaper, still excellent.
☑️ Use it for grammar, summaries, short answers.

4. REASON → Extended Thinking
☑️ Toggle it on for hard problems.
☑️ Turn it off for simple ones (saves tokens).

5. BUILD → Cowork
☑️ Where serious work happens. .docx, .xlsx, .pptx.
☑️ Point it at a folder. It works like an employee.

6. SHIP → Claude Code
☑️ For developers (and curious non-devs).
☑️ Build websites and apps from your terminal.

7. MODEL → Claude in Excel
☑️ Install the add-in from the Excel store.
☑️ Real formulas, real models, no copy-paste.

8. SLIDES → Gamma connector
☑️ One prompt. Full presentation. With research.
☑️ Connect Gamma inside Claude.

9. MOCKUP → Claude Design
☑️ Go to claude .ai/design. Build websites in 1 hour.
☑️ Upload a DESIGN .md. Export to Canva, PDF.

10. BROWSE → Claude in Chrome
☑️ A browsing agent. Reads any page for you.
☑️ Acts inside the browser, not just talks about it.

11. CLICK → Computer Use
☑️ Settings > Turn it on.
☑️ Claude clicks, types, browses on your Mac.

12. REMOTE → Dispatch
☑️ Connect your phone. Text a task.
☑️ It works on your computer while you're away.

13. SEARCH → Web Search
☑️ Click the '+'. turn it on.
☑️ It reads the live web before answering.

14. INVESTIGATE → Research
☑️ For deep multi-source reports.
☑️ 13 minutes. 50+ sources. One markdown file.

15. TRIGGER → Skills
☑️ "Use the skill-creator to build a skill for [task]."
☑️ Type /skill. It fires automatically.

16. STACK → Plugins
☑️ Cowork > Customize > Browse Plugins.
☑️ Bundles of skills for marketing, legal, sales.

17. PERSIST → Projects
☑️ One Project per recurring deliverable.
☑️ Upload one gold-standard example.

18. AUTOMATE → Scheduled Tasks
☑️ Every Monday at 7am, Claude runs your report.
☑️ Set it up once. No manual work. 

19. CONNECT → Connectors
☑️ Gmail, Drive, Slack, Notion, Granola, Calendar.
☑️ 50+ apps. Claude reads, writes & acts inside.

20. RENDER → Artifacts
☑️ Side pane where Claude opens charts, docs.
☑️ Interactive. Editable. Shareable.

21. VOICE → Custom Styles
☑️ Set "Concise" or build your own.
☑️ Saves you from re-explaining your voice.

22. SHARE → Team Projects
☑️ Same Project. Whole team inside.
☑️ Skills, files, instructions. Shared once.

23. GUARD → Global Instructions
☑️ Settings > Cowork > Global Instructions.
☑️ Tell Claude what to always do.

24. ORGANIZE → Obsidian Vault
☑️ Free app. Opens your Cowork folder neatly.
☑️ Edit files like Google Docs.

25. BRIEF → about-me file.
☑️ One md. file. Under 2,000 words.
☑️ Your style, your rules, your voice

Don’t just build them.

For each project:
→ Write a Guide
→ Push it to GitHub
→ Write what you learned
→ Record a small demo
→ Share it on X
→ Explain the problem it solves
→ Deploy some of them
→ Add screenshots if possible
→ Mention the stack you used

Diffusion policy-gradient methods for RL optimize the ELBO variational surrogate instead of the true log-likelihood. This allows the ELBO to drift from the true objective during optimization, producing misaligned gradients and unstable training. 

DiPOD from UCB proposes a direct fix: interleave self-distillation with policy-improving gradient updates and augment each diffusion policy-gradient step with an on-policy ELBO regularizer. This keeps the surrogate tightly bound to the true log-likelihood throughout training, stabilizing optimization and achieving higher rewards in robotics and control tasks.

Diffusion Policy Optimization without Drifting Apart 
Paper: https://t.co/IMSyHUMeXz

I disagree with this decision and I don't like it.

But also...

HOW DID ANTHROPIC NOT SEE THIS COMING‽

It is *the* obvious response to "this is too dangerous for anyone except us to use", since that relies on a premise ("we are uniquely good") that almost no-one agrees with.

BTW, in case you're wondering just how sports-mad us Aussies are:
Based on average attendance, men's soccer is only the 5th most popular sport in Australia.
